briefs.title: Burnaby Approves Housing Authority's 2026-2030 Strategic Plan.
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation’s (BHA) Strategic Plan for 2026-2030. The plan outlines the BHA's goals and strategies for providing affordable housing in Burnaby over the next five years. Council voted unanimously in favour of the plan.
briefs.source_body:
7.1 
BHA - 2026-2030 STRATEGIC PLAN 
The General Manager Planning and Development submitted a report seeking 
Council approval of the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation’s (BHA) 2026-
2030 Strategic Plan. 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 144 
THAT the 2026-2030 Strategic Plan of the Burnaby Housing Authority 
Corporation, substantially in the form attached as Attachment 1 to the report 
titled "BHA – 2026-2030 Strategic Plan”, dated March 24, 2026, and as described 
in section 3.1 of the report, be approved. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby Approved UBCM Membership Dues Payment for 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved paying the Union of British Columbia Municipalities (UBCM) membership dues for 2026. The payment totals $36,250.57, including taxes. UBCM is an organization that represents local governments in BC.
briefs.source_body:
7.2 
UBCM 2026 MEMBERSHIP DUES 
The General Manager Corporate Services submitted a report seeking Council 
approval for payment of the Union of British Columbia Municipalities (UBCM) 
2026 membership dues. 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 145 
THAT payment of the invoice from the Union of BC Municipalities for the 2026 
membership dues in the amount of $36,250.57, including taxes, be approved. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby approves $4.2 million contract for new water metering technology.
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved a $4.2 million contract with Neptune Technology Group Canada Co. to supply and deliver new water metering technology. The final payment will depend on the actual amount of services and unit prices provided.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant
briefs.source_body:
7.3 
CA - SUPPLY AND DELIVERY WATER METERING TECHNOLOGY 
The Deputy General Manager Finance submitted a report seeking Council 
approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the Supply and Delivery of Water 
Metering Technology. 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 146 
THAT a contract award to Neptune Technology Group Canada Co. for an 
estimated total cost of $4,200,000 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled 
“CA – Supply and Delivery of Water Metering Technology” dated March 24, 2026 
be approved; and 
THAT final payment be based on the actual quantities of services delivered and 
unit prices as proposed. 
CARRIED 
(Opposed: Councillors Calendino, Dhaliwal and Lee)

briefs.title: Burnaby refers modular housing four-plex development at 8315 14th Avenue back to staff.
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted to refer the modular four-plex housing unit development at 8315 14th Avenue back to staff. Council wants staff to review the project further to ensure project management is efficient. A modular four-plex is a building with four homes built in a factory and assembled on-site.
briefs.source_body:
7.4 
CA - DEVELOPMENT OF MODULAR HOUSING FOUR-PLEX UNIT AT 8315 
14TH AVENUE 
The Deputy General Manager Finance submitted a report seeking Council 
approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the development of a modular four-plex 
housing unit as a pilot project. 
Arising from discussion, the original recommendation was not voted on, and 
Councillor Gu introduced the following motion: 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 147 
THAT the report titled “CA – Development of Modular housing Four-Plex Unit at 
8315 14th Avenue”, dated March 24, 2026, be now REFERRED back to staff for 
further review to ensure project management efficiencies. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby Approved Contracts for Hired Equipment Services with 19 Contractors.
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved awarding contracts to 19 different contractors for hired equipment services. The final payment to each contractor will be based on the amount of service provided and the agreed-upon unit prices. This ensures the City has access to necessary equipment for various projects.
briefs.source_body:
7.5 
CA - HIRED EQUIPMENT SERVICES 
The Deputy General Manager Finance submitted a report seeking Council 
approval to award nineteen separate contracts (CA) for Hired Equipment 
Services. 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 148 
THAT a contract award to 19 separate contractors, as outlined in the report titled 
“CA - Hired Equipment Services” dated March 24, 2026, be approved; and 
THAT final payment will be based on actual quantity of services delivered and 
unit prices as submitted. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby approved contract renewal for asphalt paving services with Blackrete Paving.
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved renewing a contract with Blackrete Paving Ltd. for asphalt paving services. The contract is estimated to cost $1,155,000, including taxes. The final payment will be based on the actual amount of work done and the agreed-upon prices.
briefs.source_body:
7.6 
CR - ASPHALT PAVING SERVICES - ENGINEERING DEPARTMENT 
The Deputy General Manager Finance submitted a report seeking Council 
approval for a Contract Renewal (CR) for Asphalt Paving Services – Engineering 
Department. 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 149 
THAT a contract renewal to Blackrete Paving Ltd., for an estimated cost of 
$1,155,000 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled “CR – Asphalt Paving 
Services – Engineering Department” dated March 24, 2026 be approved; and 
THAT final payment will be based on actual quantities of services delivered and 
contracted unit prices. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby to Consider High-Density Mixed-Use Development on Carrigan Court.
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council authorized staff to work with an applicant on a plan to build a high-density mixed-use building with commercial spaces, strata (condo) units, and rental apartments at 3856 and 3870 Carrigan Court. A detailed report will come to Council later.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
briefs.source_body:
8.1 
PDC - AMENDED DEVELOPMENT PROPOSAL ENQUIRY FOR 3856 AND 
3870 CARRIGAN COURT - PROPOSED AMENDED HIGH-DENSITY MIXED-
USE COMMERCIAL/RESIDENTIAL STRATA AND RENTAL DEVELOPMENT 
The Planning and Development Committee (PDC) submitted a report seeking 
Council authorization to work with the applicant towards the preparation of a 
suitable plan of development, in accordance with Section 3.0 of this report, on 
the understanding that a more detailed report will be submitted for Council 
consideration at a later date. 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 150 
THAT staff be authorized to accept a rezoning application and to continue to 
work with the applicant towards the preparation of a suitable plan of development 
for advancement to Council, in accordance with Section 3.0 of the report titled 
“Amended Development Proposal Enquiry For 3856 and 3870 Carrigan Court – 
Proposed Amended High Density Mixed-Use Commercial/Residential Strata and 
Rental Development”, dated March 4, 2026, of the Planning and Development 
Committee meeting, for the development of an amended high density mixed-use 
commercial/residential strata and rental development, on the understanding that 
a more detailed report will be submitted for Council consideration at a later date. 
CARRIED 
(Opposed: Councillor Dhaliwal)

briefs.title: Burnaby advances rezoning for Grand Promenade to add hotel, restaurant uses.
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council authorized a rezoning bylaw for 3900 Grand Promenade. The rezoning will amend a previous plan to allow hotel and restaurant uses instead of some office space. It also adds 362 market strata units, increasing the height of two towers.
briefs.industries: Hotel & Accommodation, Food Service, Construction & Development
briefs.source_body:
8.2 
PDC - REZ #24-23 - 3900 GRAND PROMENADE, 9850 AUSTIN RD, AND 9858 
AND 9898 GATINEAU PLACE - AMENDED MIXED-USE 
COMMERCIAL/RESIDENTIAL DEVELOPMENT 
The Planning and Development Committee (PDC) submitted a report seeking 
Council authorization to forward REZ #24-23 to First and Second Reading at a 
future Council meeting. The purpose of the proposed zoning bylaw amendment 
is to amend the previous rezoning of the subject lands pursuant to REZ #18-32 
(Bylaw No. 14447), which received Final Adoption on September 23, 2024, and 
included two market strata residential buildings of 80 (Tower 1B) and 73 (Tower 
1A) storeys, respectively, inclusive of a 13-storey retail and office podium. As part 
of the subject amendment rezoning application, the applicant is proposing to 
replace a portion of the commercial office use with hotel and restaurant uses, 
and add 362 additional market strata units from the development concept 
approved under REZ #18-32, resulting in an additional seven storeys for Tower 
1B and four storeys for Tower 1A. In addition, the applicant is proposing to 
provide the inclusionary requirement associated with Phase 1 only, to be 
transferred to the Carrigan site, which is an amendment from the previous 
rezoning approval. 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 151 
THAT a Rezoning Bylaw for REZ #24-23 be prepared and advanced to First and 
Second Reading at a future Council meeting; 
THAT the predecessor Rezoning Bylaw, Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, 
Amendment Bylaw No. 6, 2025 (Bylaw No. 14737), be abandoned contingent 
upon the granting by Council of Second Reading of the subject Rezoning Bylaw; 
THAT a replacement Housing Agreement be authorized for the Carrigan site 
(3856 and 3870 Carrigan Court – REZ #20-18) according to the amended terms 
outlined in Section 3.2 of the report titled “REZ #24-23 – 3900 Grand 
Promenade, 9850 Austin Road, and 9858 and 9898 Gatineau Place – Amended 
Mixed-Use Commercial/Residential Development” dated March 4, 2026, of the 
Planning and Development Committee meeting, and the City Solicitor be 
authorized to bring forward a Housing Agreement Bylaw; and 
THAT the items listed in Attachment 3 to the report 6 be established as 
prerequisites to consideration of Final Adoption of the Rezoning Bylaw for REZ 
#24-23. 
CARRIED 
(Opposed: Mayor Hurley, Councillor Dhaliwal)

briefs.title: Burnaby Council OKs Rezoning Bylaw for Kingsway Mixed-Use Development
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council approved moving forward with a rezoning bylaw for a proposed 12-storey mixed-use development at 6535 Kingsway. The development will include 290 residential units and commercial space at street level. Council also authorized a Housing Agreement, if needed.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
briefs.source_body:
8.3 
PDC - REZ #25-09 - 6535 KINGSWAY - PROPOSED TWELVE-STOREY 
MIXED-USE DEVELOPMENT 
The Planning and Development Committee (PDC) submitted a report seeking 
Council authorization to forward REZ #25-09 to First and Second Reading at a 
future Council meeting. The purpose of the proposed zoning bylaw amendment 
is to permit the construction of a 12-storey mixed-use multiple-family 
development with a double-height commercial storey at street level, comprised of 
290 residential units, ground-oriented commercial uses fronting Kingsway, and 
underground parking. The proposal is consistent with the applicable OCP and the 
Edmonds Community Plan designations for the site. 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 152 
THAT a Rezoning Bylaw for REZ #25-09 be prepared and advanced to First and 
Second Reading at a future Council meeting; 
THAT a Housing Agreement, if required, be authorized according to the terms 
outlined in Section 3.4 of the report titled “REZ #25-09 – 6535 Kingsway – 
Proposed Twelve-Storey Mixed-Use Development” dated March 04, 2026, of the 
Planning and Development Committee meeting, and the City Solicitor be 
authorized to bring forward a Housing Agreement Bylaw; and 
THAT the items listed in Attachment 3 to the report be established as 
prerequisites to consideration of Final Adoption of the Rezoning Bylaw for REZ 
#25-09. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby Council OKs rezoning for Norland Avenue self-storage facility.
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved moving forward with a rezoning bylaw for 2757 Norland Avenue. The change will allow a five-story self-storage building to be built. A public hearing will not be held because the plan fits with Burnaby's Official Community Plan.
briefs.source_body:
8.4 
PDC - REZ #25-21 - 2757 NORLAND AVENUE - FIVE-STOREY SELF-
STORAGE FACILITY 
The Planning and Development Committee (PDC) submitted a report seeking 
Council authorization to forward REZ #25-21 to First and Second Reading at a 
future Council meeting. The purpose of the proposed zoning bylaw amendment 
is to permit the construction of a five-storey enclosed self-storage building. The 
proposal is being advanced in accordance with the applicable 1998 OCP policies 
and approved transitionary provisions. 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 153 
THAT a Rezoning Bylaw for REZ #25-21 be prepared and advanced to First and 
Second Reading at a future Council meeting; 
THAT a Public Hearing not be held for the Rezoning Bylaw for REZ #25-21, as it 
is consistent with the Burnaby Official Community Plan; and 
THAT the items listed in Attachment 3 to the report titled “REZ #25-21 – 2757 
Norland Avenue- Five-Storey Self-Storage Facility”, dated March 4, 2026, of the 
Planning and Development Committee meeting, be established as prerequisites 
to consideration of Final Adoption of the Rezoning Bylaw for REZ #25-21. 
CARRIED 
(Opposed: Councillor Gu)

briefs.title: Burnaby Council endorses Poverty Reduction Strategy; implementation plan to follow.
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council endorsed the Burnaby Poverty Reduction Strategy. Staff will now work with community groups to create a plan to put the strategy into action. The Mayor will ask the BC government for money to help Burnaby carry out these local poverty reduction projects.
briefs.source_body:
8.5 
SPC - BURNABY POVERTY REDUCTION STRATEGY 
The Social Planning Committee (SPC) submitted a report seeking Council 
endorsement of the Burnaby Poverty Reduction Strategy. 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 154 
THAT the Burnaby Poverty Reduction Strategy, provided as Attachment 1 to the 
report titled “Burnaby Poverty Reduction Strategy,” dated March 11, 2026, of the 
Social Planning Committee meeting, be endorsed; 
THAT staff be authorized to work with community partners to undertake the 
development of an implementation plan; and 
THAT the Mayor write a letter to the Minister of Social Development and Poverty 
Reduction advocating to re-establish an ongoing funding program in partnership 
with the Union of BC Municipalities (UBCM) to support communities in 
implementing local poverty reduction initiatives and forward a copy of this report 
and attachments. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby Council OKs First, Second Reading for Zoning Bylaw Amendment at 5058 Imperial St.
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council gave the first and second readings to Bylaw No. 14802. This bylaw amends the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965 and relates to rezoning application #25-13 for 5058 Imperial Street. A zoning bylaw controls how land can be used. Council will consider further readings at a future meeting.
briefs.source_body:
11.1.1 #14802 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 4, 2026 - 
REZ #25-13 (5058 Imperial Street) 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 160 
THAT Bylaw No. 14802 be now read a first and second time. 
CARRIED 
(Opposed: Mayor Hurley, Councillors Keithley, Lee, and Wang)

briefs.title: Burnaby Council OKs Zoning Bylaw Amendment for 6800 Lougheed Hwy
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved the first and second readings of Bylaw No. 14803. This bylaw amends the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965 and relates to rezoning application #25-18 for the property located at 6800 Lougheed Highway.
briefs.source_body:
11.1.2 #14803 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 5, 2026 - 
REZ #25-18 (6800 Lougheed Hwy) 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 159-1 
THAT Bylaw No. 14803 be now read a first and second time. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby Council Approves Zoning Bylaw Amendment for 2209 & 2225 Springer Avenue.
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved an amendment to the zoning bylaw. The amendment, Bylaw No. 14804, relates to rezoning application #25-20 for properties located at 2209 and 2225 Springer Avenue. A zoning bylaw controls land use and development in specific areas.
briefs.source_body:
11.1.3 #14804 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 6, 2026 - 
REZ #25-20 (2209 & 2225 Springer Avenue) 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 159-2 
THAT Bylaw No. 14804 be now read a first and second time. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby Council Adopts Amendment to Parking Meter and EV Charging Bylaw
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council approved an amendment to the Burnaby Parking Meter and Electric Vehicle Charging Meter Bylaw 2019. The amendment, officially Bylaw No. 14805, was read a final time and adopted unanimously by the council. This updates regulations for parking and electric vehicle charging within the city.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.1 #14805 - Burnaby Parking Meter and Electric Vehicle Charging Meter 
Bylaw 2019, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026 
Resolution No. 2026 - 161-1 
THAT Bylaw No. 14805 be now read a final time and adopted. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby Adopted Amendment Bylaw No. 2, 2026, to Fees and Charges Bylaw
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council adopted Amendment Bylaw No. 2, 2026, to the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. This bylaw outlines the fees and charges for various city services and permits. The specific changes in the amendment were not detailed in the provided text.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw for any changes affecting your business.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.2 #14806 - Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw, Amendment 
Bylaw No. 2, 2026 
Resolution No. 2026 - 161-2 
THAT Bylaw No. 14806 be now read a final time and adopted. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby Council Adopts Amendment Bylaw No. 14807 Regarding Notice Enforcement
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council approved Amendment Bylaw No. 14807. The bylaw relates to the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009. This means the city can now enforce its bylaws using a notice system, which is a way of dealing with violations without going to court.
briefs.action_required: Review Burnaby's updated Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw to ensure your business is compliant.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.3 #14807 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment 
Bylaw No. 2, 2026 
Resolution No. 2026 - 161-3 
THAT Bylaw No. 14807 be now read a final time and adopted. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby Approved Joanne Morgan as 2025 Outstanding Citizen of the Year.
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved Joanne Morgan as the city's 2025 Outstanding Citizen of the Year. The Kushiro Cup presentation will happen at the 2026 City Appreciation Dinner on May 1, 2026. Staff will contact the winner and those who nominated them.

key_dates.label: Kushiro Cup presentation at City Appreciation Dinner

briefs.source_body:
14.1 Resolution No. C2026 - 029 
THAT Joanne Morgan be approved as Burnaby’s 2025 Outstanding Citizen of the 
Year, and that the Kushiro Cup presentation take place at the 2026 City 
Appreciation Dinner on May 1, 2026; 
THAT the recommendation of the report titled “2025 Citizen of the Year – Kushiro 
Cup Award”, dated March 10, 2026, be released at the March 24, 2026, Open 
Council meeting; and 
THAT staff be authorized to contact the recipients and nominators prior to 
Council’s Open meeting. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY
